checks.a_analysis: The output contains several section titles that do not appear in the source document. Specifically, the headings 'Prompting Overview', 'About Copilot in Outlook', 'Follow Along - Copilot Chat - Optional', and 'Follow Along - Copilot in Outlook' are not found anywhere in the 21-page source PDF. These are invented organizational headers with no basis in the original content.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document contains no text matching the headings 'Prompting Overview', 'About Copilot in Outlook', or 'Follow Along'.
checks.b_evidence_output: "title": "Prompting Overview", "title": "About Copilot in Outlook", "title": "Follow Along - Copilot Chat - Optional", "title": "Follow Along - Copilot in Outlook"
checks.b_evidence_location: Section titles at indices 7, 10, 16, and 19 of the 'sections' array.

checks.a_analysis: All contact information in the output was verified against the source. The email address 'Josh.Meredith@microsoft.com' on slide 2 is correct. The various URLs including 'https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat', 'https://copilot.microsoft.com/', and 'https://outlook.office365.com/' accurately reflect the text found in the source document, including the triple-slash typo present in the original 'https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat'.
All 21 pages/slides from the source document are represented in the output JSON. Each slide's primary heading and body text have been successfully captured.
The source document does not contain any tables. Therefore, this check is not applicable and no rows are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A thorough comparison of the text within each slide shows that no significant portions of content were omitted. The bullet points, steps, and example prompts match the source material accurately.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output follows the source closely. Minor idiosyncratic spellings or typos in the source, such as 'https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat' (triple slash), 'particulate section of text' (slide 16), and 'Keep is short and sweet' (slide 17), were faithfully reproduced. There are no meaning-altering changes.

checks.a_analysis: I have examined the output for any duplicated content that does not appear duplicated in the source PDF. The document contains slides with repeating headings (e.g., 'Art of Prompting - Key Elements' on Pages 4 and 5, and 'Draft with Copilot in Outlook' on Pages 12 and 13), but these are inherent to the source presentation structure and not duplications created by the conversion process. No other paragraphs or list items appear to be duplicated.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and broken in several places. While most slide titles are correctly identified as Level 3 headings, several slides (Pages 9, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, and 19) do not contain a heading element within their 'elements' array. Instead, the titles are either stored only as section titles or are missing entirely. For example, Slide 13 (Page 13) has the title 'Draft with Copilot in Outlook' in the source, but the output names the section 'Slide 13' and completely omits the heading text from the content. This inconsistency disrupts navigation via heading structure, which is a key accessibility feature.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 13 Slide Title: Draft with Copilot in Outlook; Page 17 Slide Title: Practice : Draft with Copilot
checks.b_evidence_output: The section corresponding to Page 13 is titled 'Slide 13' and contains no heading element or the text 'Draft with Copilot in Outlook'. The section corresponding to Page 17 is titled 'Practice : Draft with Copilot' but contains no heading element in its 'elements' array.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections for Slide 9, Slide 12 (Draft with Copilot in Outlook), Slide 13, Slide 14 (Coaching by Copilot in Outlook), and Practice slides 15-19.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF does not contain any formal tables. Page 20 contains a grid of prompt cards, which has been appropriately handled as a section title and image rather than a corrupted table structure.

The output maintains the logical reading order of the source document. It follows the slide-by-slide sequence. For complex layouts like the pillars on Page 4 and the feature boxes on Page 10, the output correctly follows a left-to-right, top-to-bottom sequence that matches the intended flow of information.

Lists are generally converted correctly. Unordered bulleted lists (e.g., on Slide 3 and Slide 12) and ordered numbered lists (e.g., on Slide 6 and Slide 15) are represented as 'list' types in the JSON. The multi-part list on Page 17, which is interrupted by a prompt example, is correctly split into two list objects separated by a paragraph, preserving the semantic meaning.

The output fails to identify numerous URLs as active link objects. While the email address on Page 2 is correctly formatted with an 'href', several URLs visible in the PDF (e.g., 'https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat' on Page 6 and 'https://outlook.office365.com/' on Page 16) are rendered as plain text strings rather than interactive link objects with 'href' attributes. This results in missing navigational functionality.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "Go to Copilot in Teams or https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat"; "text": "Go to Outlook or https://outlook.office365.com/"
checks.b_evidence_location: Slide 6, Slide 7, Slide 15, Slide 16, Slide 17, Slide 18

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the slide-based content into a linear, responsive format. The logical flow and organization of the information (slides presented in sequence) match the source PDF. While complex multi-column layouts (like the boxes on page 4 or page 20) are flattened into single-column lists, this is an expected behavior of responsive HTML conversion. A user can find the same information in the same relative sequence.
The source document does not contain any formal data tables. It uses text blocks and images to present information. Therefore, no tables were misrendered.

Multiple meaningful images from the source PDF are missing or broken in the HTML rendering. On page 2, the group photo and the surfing photo are replaced by green placeholder blocks. Significant diagrams on pages 4 (icons) and 5 (annotated prompt) are missing. Critical software screenshots that provide visual context for the instructions on pages 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14 are also missing, represented only by green blocks. Finally, the grid of prompt cards on page 20 is completely missing. These images are central to the instructional content of the presentation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains meaningful photos (group photo, surfboard photo on page 2), diagrams (annotated prompt on page 5, icons on page 4), and instructional screenshots (pages 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 20).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the rendered HTML, these elements are absent and replaced by green placeholder blocks (e.g., Slide 2 shows only two of four photos; Slide 4, 5, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 20 show large green blocks instead of content images).

checks.a_analysis: Review of the rendered HTML output shows no evidence of duplicated body content. Each slide's text and remaining images appear exactly once in the document flow, following the navigation structure.
